perturbation.original_text: The Client will hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose, copy, reproduce or distribute it to any person other than its Authorised Recipients (defined as the Client ’s directors, officers, employees, associates, affiliates and advisers who are directly concerned with the Transaction and whose knowledge o f such Confidential Information is necessary for the Transaction ).
perturbation.changed_text: The Customer will maintain the Sensitive Data in reasonable confidence and will not share, replicate, or disseminate it to any individual other than its Approved Parties (defined as the Customer’s executives, managers, personnel, partners, and consultants who are directly involved with the Deal and whose knowledge of such Sensitive Data is advisable for the Deal).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Client' to 'Customer', 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data', 'disclose, copy, reproduce or distribute' to 'share, replicate, or disseminate' and 'Authorised Recipients' to 'Approved Parties' introduces a perturbation by creating ambiguity and weakening the obligation. The new wording, especially 'reasonable confidence' and 'advisable', is less strict than the original, potentially leading to disputes. Additionally, the altered terms for roles might lead to contradictions regarding which individuals are bound by the agreement. The change to the terms, make a contradiction in the level of information being passed, since sensitive data is more broad.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. The Client agrees to ensure that each of the Authorised Recipients adheres to the terms set forth in this NDA and acknowledges that it shall be liable for any damages incurred in connection with the breach of its obligations. This NDA shall be governed by, and construed in accordance of German law, and each party irrevocably submits to the exclusive jurisdiction of the German courts. Both parties agree that a signed , facsimile copy shall have the same force and effect and as the original NDA
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Client' to 'Customer', 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Data', and stricter terms like 'strict confidence' and 'disclose' to weaker terms like 'reasonable confidence' and 'share', alongside the altered definition of recipients, introduces a contradiction. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'The Client agrees to ensure that each of the Authorised Recipients adheres to the terms set forth in this NDA' and holds the client liable for breaches. By weakening the obligations and changing the definitions, the altered text undermines the enforceability of the original NDA terms on the 'Approved Parties' handling 'Sensitive Data', creating a contradiction regarding the standard of care and liability as described in the contradicted section. The change to 'Sensitive Data', which is broader than 'Confidential Information', also contradicts the original text, since the original text does not account for the new potentially expanded scope.

perturbation.original_text: Neither the Client nor the Authorised Recipients will, without the prior consent of Vladi Private Islands: a. reveal to any person other than an Authorised Recipient that negotiations are taking place in relation to the Transaction or reveal any other details concerning the status of negotiations b. use the Confidential Information for any purpose other t han to evaluate the Transaction
perturbation.changed_text: Neither the Customer nor the Designated Individuals will, without explicit approval from Vladi Private Islands: a. communicate to anyone beyond a Designated Individual that talks are occurring regarding the Project or communicate any other specifics regarding the state of discussions b. utilize the Private Details for any reason other than to assess the Project
perturbation.explanation: Switching 'Client' to 'Customer', 'Authorised Recipients' to 'Designated Individuals', 'negotiations' to 'talks', 'Transaction' to 'Project', and 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Details' creates confusion regarding the scope and nature of the obligations. The terms 'explicit approval', 'talks', 'Project', and 'Private Details' aren't precise and could lead to disputes about what activities are covered by the NDA. This contradicts the other sections referencing 'Client' and 'Transaction'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. All correspondence, inquiries, offers to purchase and negotiations relating to the Transaction will be conducted exclusively through Vladi Private Islands. The Client and Authorised Recipients agree only to contact the property owner and/or the affiliates of the property owner through Vladi Private Islands .
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Client' with 'Customer', 'Authorised Recipients' with 'Designated Individuals', and 'Transaction' with 'Project'. The contradicted text explicitly states that "The Client and Authorised Recipients agree only to contact the property owner and/or the affiliates of the property owner through Vladi Private Islands" and references the 'Transaction'. Since the changed text uses different terms, it creates uncertainty regarding whether the restrictions in the contradicted text still apply, thus creating a contradiction. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, where interchangeable use of terms leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All correspondence, inquiries, offers to purchase and negotiations relating to the Transaction will be conducted exclusively through Vladi Private Islands. The Client and Authorised Recipients agree only to contact the property owner and/or the affiliates of the property owner through Vladi Private Islands .
perturbation.changed_text: All communications, questions, bids to acquire, and discussions concerning the Deal must occur solely via Vladi Private Islands. The Customer and Approved Personnel agree to only reach out to the property possessor and/or the property possessor’s associated entities through Vladi Private Islands.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'correspondence' with 'communications', 'inquiries' with 'questions', 'offers to purchase' with 'bids to acquire', 'negotiations' with 'discussions', 'Transaction' with 'Deal', 'Client' with 'Customer', 'Authorised Recipients' with 'Approved Personnel', 'property owner' with 'property possessor', and 'affiliates' with 'associated entities', introduces confusion. The differing wording creates uncertainty as to the precise nature and scope of restricted interactions, leading to ambiguity about what constitutes a breach of contract. The other sections are contradicted since they reffered to other kind of interactions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. The obligation of the client set forth in this NDA shall terminate 2 years afte r its creation and shall remain effective in the event that the Client terminates its co -operation with Vla di Private Islands.
perturbation.justification: The explanation states that the change introduces confusion due to differing wording ('correspondence' to 'communications', etc.). This creates uncertainty regarding restricted interactions and what constitutes a breach. The original text defines 'client' and related terms. The change modifies these, and the contradicted text refers specifically to 'the client'. Because the modified text redefines what 'the client' is, and renames other previously named terms, it creates ambiguity in the document, especially because of the use of a particular term in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The obligation of the client set forth in this NDA shall terminate 2 years afte r its creation and shall remain effective in the event that the Client terminates its co -operation with Vla di Private Islands.
perturbation.changed_text: The duties of the user outlined in this Confidentiality Agreement will end 2 years after its establishment and will still be in effect if the user ends their collaboration with Vladi Private Islands.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'obligation of the client' to 'duties of the user', 'NDA' to 'Confidentiality Agreement', and 'Client' to 'user', creates uncertainty regarding the exact nature of the commitment and who is bound by it. It contradicts all previous mentions of 'Client' and 'NDA' and creates ambiguity as to whether these obligations remain legally enforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. The Client will hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and will not disclose, copy, reproduce or distribute it to any person other than its Authorised Recipients (defined as the Client ’s directors, officers, employees, associates, affiliates and advisers who are directly concerned with the Transaction and whose knowledge o f such Confidential Information is necessary for the Transaction ).
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'obligation of the client' with 'duties of the user' and 'Client' with 'user', while also renaming 'NDA' to 'Confidentiality Agreement'. The contradicted text explicitly states 'The Client will hold the Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the changed text because 'Client' and 'user' are now different terms, creating ambiguity as to who is responsible for maintaining confidentiality as defined earlier in the contract. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition as the multiple terms used interchangeably without clear definition leads to conflicting obligations.
